title: Lethbridge Police Need Your Help Finding a Man Wanted for Sexual Assault
excerpt: Lethbridge Police are asking the public for help finding Jonathan Milton Janes, 33, wanted on warrants for sexual assault and breaching a sentencing order, and this isn't his first time evading police.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Winter storm hits Calgary, expected to continue into evening
excerpt: After weeks of unusually mild weather, Calgary got hit with a reality check Tuesday. A powerful winter storm swept through the city bringing heavy snow, near-zero visibility, and wind chills that dropped to -31°C — the kind of day that reminds you this is still Alberta in February.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Calgary Bobcat Sightings Double as Wild Cats Lose Their Shyness
excerpt: Calgarians are spotting more bobcats than ever in residential neighborhoods, and experts say these wild cats are no longer acting like the "shy" and "elusive" animals they're supposed to be. Sightings have doubled from around 1,000 reports per year before 2018 to a steady 2,000 annual sightings.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Winter Isn't Done With Calgary Yet: Up to 30 cm of Snow Expected to Hit
excerpt: Calgary could see significant snowfall starting Monday, with blowing snow and poor visibility expected across major highways.

title: A Calgary Family Is Searching For Answers After Their 17-Year-Old Son Disappeared Without His Phone, Wallet, Or ID
excerpt: A 17-year-old Edgemont teen left home early Sunday morning without his phone, wallet, or ID. Days later, there's still no word from him.

title: Lethbridge Boy, 13, Gets Max Youth Sentence for Stabbing His 7-Year-Old Brother
excerpt: The attack left the younger boy with life-threatening wounds. The older brother is now entering a rehab-focused program the toughest sentence youth court can hand down.

title: Calgary Police Seek Public's Help After Attempted Child Abduction in Coventry Hills
excerpt: An 11-year-old boy managed to escape after a man tried to force him to leave with him while he was riding his bike in northeast Calgary.

excerpt: Two women executed a sophisticated payment fraud scheme targeting a Vietnamese family's jewelry business, making off with thousands in gold in Calgary.

title: Calgary Water Main Breaks Again, Thousands Under Boil Water Advisory
excerpt: Calgary's Bearspaw South Feeder Main has burst again, leaving thousands under a boil water advisory. Firefighters rescued 13 people from flooded vehicles on the Trans-Canada Highway last night.

title: Man Exposed Himself to Mother and Children on Calgary Transit Bus
excerpt: A Calgary mother and her children witnessed a man expose himself on a Route 17 transit bus near Erlton on. Police are asking the public for help identifying the suspect.

title: Woman Dies After Falling Into Deep Snow at Banff Ski Resort
excerpt: A Toronto woman died at Banff's Sunshine Village after falling into deep powder on a beginner run. Ski patrol worked for two hours to save her. Here's what every skier needs to know about snow immersion.

title: Calgary Says Goodbye to Toys R Us: The End of an Era on Macleod Trail
excerpt: Calgary's last Toys R Us on Macleod Trail closed on Boxing Day, leaving the city without the iconic toy store. The nearest location is now CrossIron Mills
author: Culture Alberta

title: Skier in Stable Condition After 12-Metre Fall From Banff Sunshine Chairlift
excerpt: An 18-year-old woman is in stable condition after falling 12 metres from a chairlift at Banff Sunshine Village on Sunday. Photos of the incident quickly went viral on Reddit, showing the woman hanging from the lift before the fall. The popular Alberta ski resort says it's investigating.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Alberta Independence Referendum Petition Gets Green Light From Elections Alberta
excerpt: Elections Alberta has approved a citizen initiative petition for an Alberta independence referendum. Organizers have four months to collect 178,000 signatures and some Americans are already hoping Alberta joins the U.S. as the 51st state
author: Culture Alberta

title: Reddit Calgary's Top 5 Must-Try Restaurants for Calgarian Foodies
excerpt: Skip the tourist traps, Calgary's reddit community has spoken. From Model Milk (Pedro Pascal's Calgary favourite) to Shiki Menya's cult-status ramen, these five restaurants are where locals actually eat. Here's what to order and why they're worth the hype.

title: Calgary Police Issue Rare "Do Not Travel" Advisory as Winter Storm Batters City

title: Calgary's Most Iconic Public Art Sculptures
excerpt: Calgary's streets are full of public art you've probably walked past a hundred times. From the bronze businessmen on Stephen Avenue to the controversial $471,000 Blue Ring near Deerfoot, here's the story behind five sculptures that define the city

title: Calgary Nationalist Group Drops "Remigration" Banner Over Deerfoot
excerpt: A nationalist group staged its first Alberta demonstration Saturday, dropping a "Remigration Now" banner over a Deerfoot Highway overpass. The Dominion Society of Canada, founded in July, has been pushing for mass deportations and a 10-year freeze on immigration
author: Culture Alberta

title: Space Debris Lit Up Calgary Skies — And Thousands Stopped to Watch
excerpt: A fireball lit up Alberta skies Sunday evening but it wasn't a meteor. University of Calgary astronomer Jeroen Stil says it was likely rocket debris from a New Zealand launch burning up on re-entry
author: Culture Alberta

title: How Calgary Became Home to Canada's Largest Mosque
excerpt: There's a stretch of northeast Calgary where the prairie sky meets something unexpected a 97-foot steel minaret rising above the suburban rooftops of Castleridge. This isn't Dubai or Istanbul.

title: Immigration Detainee Escapes Calgary Hospital, CBSA Investigation Ongoing

title: NEON YYC: Lighting Up Calgary With Custom Signage
excerpt: Calgary is lighting up with creativity thanks to NEON YYC. Specializing in custom signage for businesses, events, and homes, NEON YYC combines local craftsmanship with bold design to create eye-catching displays across the city. Trusted by top Calgary brands like TELUS Spark, BarBurrito, and Righteous Gelato, they turn ideas into glowing statement pieces made right here in Calgary
author: Culture Alberta

title: Man Performs Nazi Salute at Chinook Centre, Calgary
excerpt: Calgary — An alarming incident unfolded at CF Chinook Centre in Calgary recently, when a man was captured on video making a Nazi salute in the mall’s public area.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Jeromy Farkas Officially Declared Calgary’s New Mayor After Recount
excerpt: After a week of uncertainty, a recount has officially confirmed Jeromy Farkas as Calgary’s new mayor, edging out rival Sonya Sharp by just 616 votes. With one of the closest races in Calgary’s history and turnout under 40%, Farkas now faces the challenge of uniting a divided electorate as he prepares to be sworn in on October 29 alongside a largely new city council.
author: Culture Alberta
